gaps.summary: Adding 3 new LifecyclePhase events to the shared Event._REGISTRY broke an M01 test that asserted exactly 13 entries. The full sw-verify.sh suite failed (1605 ran, 1 failure).
gaps.fix: Replaced the exact-count assertion with three subset checks: M01 events must be present, M02 lifecycle events must be present when lifecycle_events.py is imported, and no unexpected entries are allowed. Future milestones must extend EXPECTED_EVENT_TYPES / LIFECYCLE_M02_EVENT_TYPES when adding new event families.

gaps.summary: When a phase-4 sprint-delegate node is dispatched but `sprint_delegate` is None, the runner persisted NodeState.RUNNING to disk and emitted LifecyclePhaseStarted before raising RunnerError. The node was then stuck in RUNNING (never reschedulable) with no live process.
gaps.fix: Moved the sprint_delegate-is-None precondition check to BEFORE the RUNNING transition (and before incrementing attempts). RunnerError now raises while the node is still PENDING. Added regression test `test_phase4_without_delegate_does_not_leave_node_running`.

gaps.summary: Docstring claimed `A flaky sink must never break a run`, but only OSError was caught. A TypeError from a malformed dataclass, ValueError from JSON serialization, or any other emitter-side bug would abort the runner's state-transition contract.
gaps.fix: Broadened the catch to Exception (intentionally not BaseException, so KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit still propagate). Log line now includes exception type for diagnosability. Added regression test `test_emit_failure_with_non_oserror_does_not_crash_runner`.

gaps.summary: When sprint_delegate or verifier_dispatch returned a non-dict value, the runner raised RunnerError mid-run. Status file remained at RUNNING with LifecyclePhaseStarted emitted and no matching Failed event.
gaps.fix: Converted both branches to graceful `_fail()` paths with distinct error_class strings (`DelegateInvalidResult`, `VerifierInvalidResult`). Status file transitions to FAILED with a descriptive last_error; LifecyclePhaseFailed is emitted normally; the run can continue.

gaps.summary: Only `/` was replaced with `-`. tmux session names also reject `.`, `:`, and whitespace. The policy loader only enforces non-empty node IDs, so any node ID outside `[A-Za-z0-9_-]` would crash the spawn with an opaque tmux error far from the root cause.
gaps.fix: Sanitization now uses a regex (`[^A-Za-z0-9_-]` → `-`) covering all tmux-unsafe characters. Added regression test `SessionNameSanitizationTests.test_disallowed_chars_replaced_with_dash`.

gaps.summary: `lifecycle_events` and `run_identity` were imported inside `run_next_node`, so the auto-registration of LifecyclePhase{Started,Completed,Failed} did not happen until the first invocation. If something imported the runner module but never invoked it, a JSONL parser would route lifecycle event lines to UnknownEvent.
gaps.fix: Hoisted both imports to module top, plus the lazy `from datetime import datetime` in `_duration`. Registration is now deterministic at module load.
